completion_rule: Planning never counts as implementation, runtime, artifact, engine, model, workflow, mask, QA, promotion, or release completion.

requirements.title: Modular Media Program Charter
requirements.implementation_action: Freeze the external control plane, modular ComfyUI execution boundary, App Mode role, registry authority, MaskFactory boundary, and monolithic-graph prohibition.
requirements.acceptance: Every component has one explicit responsibility and contract; the package extends rather than rewrites Rows001-148.

requirements.title: Decision and Promotion Authority Matrix
requirements.implementation_action: Assign proposal, validation, execution, QA, promotion, revocation, and rollback authority for packages, passes, attempts, and artifacts.
requirements.acceptance: Every state transition has one final authority and no LLM or VLM can self-promote.
requirements.review_method: representative image, video, and audio decision-packet review

requirements.title: Row Namespace and Non-Overlap Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Reserve Rows149-220 and bind each row to one workstream, target, deliverable, and evidence class.
requirements.acceptance: Exactly 72 unique rows exist in eighteen four-row workstreams with no completion claim or collision.

requirements.title: Revision, Exception, and Supersession Control
requirements.implementation_action: Define immutable revisions, compatibility windows, exception records, deprecation, supersession, rollback, and revocation.
requirements.acceptance: No record is silently mutated; every exception is bounded, evidence-linked, reversible, and historically traceable.

requirements.title: Canonical Entity ID and Relationship Model
requirements.implementation_action: Define durable IDs for characters, revisions, scenes, shots, takes, instances, poses, masks, passes, attempts, routes, stacks, artifacts, events, runs, and QA decisions.
requirements.acceptance: IDs are immutable, collision-tested, reference-valid, and distinguish reusable characters from shot instances.

requirements.title: Canonical Character Package Envelope
requirements.implementation_action: Define identity, morphology, surface, wardrobe, accessory, voice, reference, adapter, benchmark, rights, and QA sections.
requirements.acceptance: The package contains no fixed node IDs or character paths; all production assets and revisions are hash-bound.
requirements.review_method: reference contact sheet and voice-sample review

requirements.title: Scene, Shot, Pose, and Pass Package Envelopes
requirements.implementation_action: Define camera, framing, instance, skeleton, depth, contact, timing, audio-event, mask, protection, and pass-objective contracts.
requirements.acceptance: A pass compiles without hidden state and every target belongs to a scene, shot, take, and owner instance.
requirements.review_method: pose, depth, ownership, and event-timeline review

requirements.title: Artifact, Evidence, and Lineage Envelope
requirements.implementation_action: Define parent hashes, transforms, stack/workflow hashes, attempts, runtime bindings, QA decisions, promotion, revocation, and replay data.
requirements.acceptance: Every output is reproducible or explainable from immutable parents and version-compatible decisions.
requirements.review_method: image, video, and audio evidence-envelope review

requirements.title: Character Reference Intake and Identity Core
requirements.implementation_action: Build reference intake, view coverage, quality grading, identity traits, proportions, marks, and voice-reference binding.
requirements.acceptance: Accepted/rejected references, coverage gaps, conflicts, hashes, and authority are explicit.
requirements.review_method: face/body view grid and voice-reference listening review

requirements.title: Modular Character State System
requirements.implementation_action: Separate morphology, skin/surface, hair, makeup, wardrobe, accessory, material, and voice states from immutable identity.
requirements.acceptance: States are independently versioned, ownership-tagged, composable, and identity-preserving.
requirements.review_method: before/after state panels and relevant voice playback

requirements.title: Engine-Specific Character Adapter Builder
requirements.implementation_action: Publish FLUX-family, SDXL-family, edit, video, and audio adapter cards for one Character Package revision.
requirements.acceptance: Each adapter declares exact engine family, triggers, limits, hashes, calibration evidence, and prohibited pairings.

requirements.title: Character Revision Certification and Publication
requirements.implementation_action: Certify and publish immutable Character Package revisions for solo and multi-character consumption.
requirements.acceptance: Identity, morphology, views, states, voice, adapters, and separation pass; failures remain staged and revocable.
requirements.review_method: full character contact sheet, separation panel, and voice-continuity review

requirements.title: Scene-to-Shot and Pose Package Compiler
requirements.implementation_action: Compile intent into camera, lens, framing, per-instance keypoints/keyframes, timing, safe margins, and expected audio events.
requirements.acceptance: One reusable package serves image, mask, video, audio, and AV without flattening ownership.
requirements.review_method: skeleton, camera, framing, and timing-overlay review

requirements.title: Per-Instance Ownership and Isolation Registry
requirements.implementation_action: Assign every instance its character revision, skeleton, depth, silhouette, masks, protected regions, objects, wardrobe, and voice.
requirements.acceptance: No edit, mask, prop, dialogue line, control, or artifact is ambiguously owned.

requirements.title: Contact, Occlusion, and Depth Interaction Planner
requirements.implementation_action: Encode person/person, person/object, body/support, wardrobe, visibility, occlusion, collision, and timed contact.
requirements.acceptance: Every contact declares participants, regions, timing, force class, visibility, ownership, and expected deformation.
requirements.review_method: contact, depth, pressure, and occlusion review

requirements.title: Pose and Ownership Conformance Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Validate outputs against pose, framing, ownership, contact, and protected-region contracts and issue scoped repairs.
requirements.acceptance: Wrong-person edits, swaps, pose drift, ownership leakage, and ambiguous occlusion block promotion.

requirements.title: Unified Per-Pass Capability Registry
requirements.implementation_action: Register exact engine/model/adapter capabilities by modality, intent, region, controls, references, edit mode, resolution, hardware, and evidence.
requirements.acceptance: A one-engine specialist is discoverable only for its certified pass scope and never becomes a universal default.

requirements.title: Hard Compatibility and Constraint Solver
requirements.implementation_action: Reject engine, model, VAE, encoder, latent, adapter, control, node, workflow, license, hardware, authority, and package incompatibilities before ranking.
requirements.acceptance: No incompatible stack reaches execution; every rejection returns typed unmet constraints and missing evidence.

requirements.title: Contextual Candidate Ranking and LLM Proposal
requirements.implementation_action: Rank only eligible stacks by matching quality, preservation, stability, failure rate, runtime, memory, cost, and cache evidence.
requirements.acceptance: Ranking inputs are replayable and explainable; an LLM proposal cannot bypass compatibility or promotion gates.
requirements.review_method: blinded side-by-side media review for rank calibration

requirements.title: Fallback, Abstention, and Route Decision Record
requirements.implementation_action: Define explicit fallback, abstention, material-hypothesis retry, supersession, and immutable route-decision provenance.
requirements.acceptance: No eligible stack yields a typed blocker; every reroute explains the prior failure and no silent substitution or seed loop occurs.
requirements.review_method: fallback media review only when certifying fallback quality

requirements.title: First-Pass Intent Classifier and Engine Selector
requirements.implementation_action: Classify composition, identity, pose, edit, character-count, motion, downstream specialist, quality, and resource needs before base routing.
requirements.acceptance: The first pass is objective-driven and declares downstream bridge needs; FLUX is a candidate rather than a hardcoded answer.
requirements.review_method: base composition and identity comparison panels

requirements.title: Canonical Decoded Artifact Bridge
requirements.implementation_action: Standardize decoded image/frame/audio transfer, color/sample space, alpha, dimensions, coordinates, timebase, masks, crops, transforms, and hashes.
requirements.acceptance: Cross-engine round trips preserve coordinates, timing, color, ownership, and lineage; latent transfer blocks absent exact certification.
requirements.review_method: image difference maps and audio alignment/null checks

requirements.title: Cross-Engine Conditioning Translator
requirements.implementation_action: Translate prompts, references, pose, depth, masks, controls, denoise, scheduler, and adapter semantics into engine-native contracts.
requirements.acceptance: Raw settings are never copied blindly and every translation is versioned and testable.

requirements.title: First-Pass-to-Specialist Bridge Qualification
requirements.implementation_action: Benchmark and certify base/specialist engine pairings inside exact capability and resource buckets.
requirements.acceptance: A pairing promotes only when identity, geometry, regional fidelity, seams, preservation, and whole-artifact regression pass.

requirements.title: Specialist Pass Catalog
requirements.implementation_action: Define pass intents for face/eyes, hands/feet, anatomy, skin, hair, fabric, accessories, materials, contact, pressure, and deformation.
requirements.acceptance: Each entry binds capability tags, eligible exact stacks, inputs, outputs, preservation risks, and QA gates.

requirements.title: Regional Edit and Protected-Region Contract
requirements.implementation_action: Bind target owner/mask, protected masks, crop/pad/feather, resolution, denoise bounds, transforms, recomposition, and parent artifact.
requirements.acceptance: No localized pass executes without trusted ownership and invertible preservation constraints.
requirements.review_method: mask overlays, seams, and unaffected-region comparison

requirements.title: Defect-to-Hypothesis Repair Planner
requirements.implementation_action: Translate a classified defect into one bounded specialist hypothesis, exact route, changed variables, and retry budget.
requirements.acceptance: Retries materially differ, address the cause, preserve accepted parents, and stop on exhaustion or worsening evidence.

requirements.title: Regional Reintegration and Global Regression Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Reintegrate accepted specialist output and evaluate color, grain, sharpness, geometry, identity, ownership, temporal, and audio continuity.
requirements.acceptance: Local improvement cannot promote when any protected owner, region, span, stem, or whole artifact regresses.

requirements.title: MaskFactory Mode A Approved-Package Reader
requirements.implementation_action: Read approved packages and validate image/parent hash, person index, ontology, coordinates, transforms, mask type, truth tier, provider, and certificate.
requirements.acceptance: Only certificate-authorized masks support promotion; visible truth and derived/non-truth remain distinguished and mismatches fail closed.

requirements.title: MaskFactory Mode B Live Draft Client
requirements.implementation_action: Implement health, predict, refine, timeout, retry, champion-model, confidence, and provenance contracts for live MaskFactory access.
requirements.acceptance: Live results remain drafts at their certified authority and service absence never silently falls back to untrusted masks.

requirements.title: Normalized Mask Adapter and Arbitration Contract
requirements.implementation_action: Normalize Mode A and Mode B outputs while preserving taxonomy, transforms, owner, source, truth tier, certificate, and disagreement.
requirements.acceptance: Mode B cannot overwrite stronger Mode A authority and every union/intersection/refinement is explicitly derived.
requirements.review_method: comparative boundary and protected-region overlay review

requirements.title: Mask Availability and Promotion Integration Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Bind mask readiness to compilation, blocking, execution, repair, QA, and promotion without blocking unrelated DAG branches.
requirements.acceptance: Offline service, unknown taxonomy, ambiguous owner, stale certificate, or transform mismatch produces a typed dependent-pass blocker.

requirements.title: Dynamic Image Pass-DAG Compiler
requirements.implementation_action: Compile Character, Shot/Pose, route, bridge, specialist, and mask contracts into a minimal resumable image DAG.
requirements.acceptance: Only needed passes exist; accepted parents are immutable and dependencies, budgets, writes, protections, and gates are explicit.

requirements.title: Versioned Image Workflow Module Library
requirements.implementation_action: Parameterize composition, identity, pose/depth, realism, regional repair, material, upscale, and export workflows with stable patch points.
requirements.acceptance: Modules are API-compatible and contain no fixed character names, hidden paths, or orchestration decisions.
requirements.review_method: one certified output panel per promoted module

requirements.title: Single- and Multi-Character Image Vertical Slices
requirements.implementation_action: Execute one complete single-character run then a two-character ownership/contact run with scoped repairs.
requirements.acceptance: Both runs resume, preserve identity/ownership, and rerun only failed regions using bounded causal hypotheses.
requirements.review_method: whole-frame, per-character, pose, contact, and regional review

requirements.title: Image Artifact Reproducibility and Promotion Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Bind images to packages, prompts, seeds, models, workflows, masks, transforms, attempts, runtime, scorecards, and revocation.
requirements.acceptance: Only target/protected/whole-frame threshold-passing outputs become immutable accepted parents or promoted artifacts.

requirements.title: Approved Keyframe and Shot-to-Video Adapter
requirements.implementation_action: Convert accepted images plus character, camera, pose, depth, contact, and timing contracts into video-engine requests.
requirements.acceptance: Requests retain shot/instance authority and reject unapproved or incompatible keyframes.

requirements.title: Segment-Level Video Engine and Model Routing
requirements.implementation_action: Route generation, continuation, interpolation, motion control, and span repair independently by certified capability.
requirements.acceptance: Each segment has a route, overlaps/transitions, temporal constraints, resource envelope, and explicit fallback.
requirements.review_method: motion, transition, camera, identity, and ownership review

requirements.title: Temporal Identity and Failed-Span Repair
requirements.implementation_action: Detect identity drift, ownership swap, structural/contact errors, flicker, and discontinuity and repair only the causal span.
requirements.acceptance: Accepted spans remain immutable and repaired boundaries reconnect without temporal, identity, or timing regression.
requirements.review_method: frame strip, playback, flow, contact, and boundary review

requirements.title: Video/GIF Promotion and Export Package
requirements.implementation_action: Package final video/GIF, frames, timebase, provenance, QA, repairs, encodes, and export variants.
requirements.acceptance: Frame count, duration, FPS, loop/export, lineage, playback, and temporal scorecards pass before promotion.
requirements.review_method: mandatory complete playback and loop review

requirements.title: Shot-to-Audio Intent and Event Binding
requirements.implementation_action: Bind shot events, character voices, dialogue, contacts, environment, distance, room, and timing to existing sound and speech authorities.
requirements.acceptance: Rows067-148 are reused without duplicating their indexing, generation, voice, alignment, or certification scope.

requirements.title: Modular Audio Workflow Adapter Library
requirements.implementation_action: Compose speech, nonverbal vocalization, Foley, ambience, music, room, spatial, enhancement, and mix adapters under one contract.
requirements.acceptance: Every adapter declares exact package versions, stack requirements, stems, timebase, provenance, and QA gates.

requirements.title: Audio Execution, Alignment, and Lineage Pipeline
requirements.implementation_action: Execute bounded candidates, align events and speech, render space/room, mix buses, and preserve sample-level lineage.
requirements.acceptance: Every sample span is attributable to event/source/character/attempt/transform/mix decisions and failed stems are independently replaceable.

requirements.title: Audio Package Promotion and Revocation Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Apply identity, prosody, timing, acoustics, defect, full-audio, continuity, provenance, promotion, and revocation gates.
requirements.acceptance: Only complete synchronized packages promote and local stem gains cannot hide global audio regression.

requirements.title: Canonical AV Timeline and Clock Authority
requirements.implementation_action: Define master timebase, frame/sample conversion, cuts, dialogue, events, offsets, handles, and ownership.
requirements.acceptance: All streams resolve to one monotonic clock without cumulative drift or ambiguous event ownership.

requirements.title: Provenance-Preserving AV Assembly and Mux
requirements.implementation_action: Assemble only accepted video and audio while retaining stems, captions, metadata, codecs, hashes, and source lineage.
requirements.acceptance: Mux never substitutes unapproved media and all streams, durations, hashes, and manifests reconcile.

requirements.title: Localized AV Sync Diagnosis and Repair
requirements.implementation_action: Classify lip, event, drift, offset, source-timing, and mux defects and repair the smallest authoritative span or stem.
requirements.acceptance: Repair avoids unnecessary regeneration and always triggers complete AV regression review.
requirements.review_method: normal-speed and diagnostic slow-motion before/after review

requirements.title: Final AV Artifact Certification Package
requirements.implementation_action: Package video, audio, stems, timeline, captions, hashes, QA, playback, decisions, and rollback/revocation instructions.
requirements.acceptance: Independent synchronization, identity, continuity, technical, provenance, and full-playback gates all pass.
requirements.review_method: mandatory end-to-end visual and audio review

requirements.title: Planner, Validator, Executor, and Promoter Separation
requirements.implementation_action: Implement explicit services for proposal, deterministic validation, routing, scheduling, execution, QA observation, policy, and promotion.
requirements.acceptance: No service silently assumes another authority and every contract/state transition is versioned and replayable.

requirements.title: Immutable Run and Event Store Schema
requirements.implementation_action: Persist jobs, runs, DAGs, passes, edges, attempts, hypotheses, artifacts, routes, blockers, QA, promotion, and rollback events.
requirements.acceptance: Append-only events reconstruct exact projections with referential integrity, hashes, idempotency, and no lost terminal state.
requirements.review_method: artifact previews and playback linked from reconstructed state

requirements.title: Resumable DAG Scheduler and Hypothesis Retry Engine
requirements.implementation_action: Schedule ready passes, enforce dependency/resource/retry budgets, classify failures, and choose material repair hypotheses.
requirements.acceptance: Crash or failure resumes without duplicate promotion, unexplained seed loops, accepted-parent mutation, or successful-branch reruns.

requirements.title: Runtime Reconciliation and Recovery Controller
requirements.implementation_action: Reconcile event store, ComfyUI queue/history, files/S3, caches, locks, artifacts, QA, and promotion after interruption.
requirements.acceptance: Orphans, partial writes, missing outputs, stale locks, duplicate delivery, and conflicts resolve fail-closed and idempotently.

requirements.title: Self-Hosted Model Role and Authority Decomposition
requirements.implementation_action: Separate planner, prompt composer, router advisor, defect classifier, VLM critic, audio critic, retrieval, and summarizer roles.
requirements.acceptance: Every role has bounded contracts, context, model requirements, escalation, uncertainty, and no promotion authority.
requirements.review_method: critic output review against known media cases

requirements.title: Registry-Grounded Retrieval and Tool Context
requirements.implementation_action: Build retrieval over schemas, packages, capabilities, workflows, benchmarks, failures, evidence, and current event state.
requirements.acceptance: Responses cite immutable IDs and surface stale, conflicting, missing, or out-of-scope evidence rather than inventing it.

requirements.title: Structured Proposal, Uncertainty, and Validation Contract
requirements.implementation_action: Require schema-valid plans, prompts, routes, diagnoses, hypotheses, confidence, evidence references, and alternatives.
requirements.acceptance: Unsupported IDs and authority bypasses reject deterministically; uncertainty may abstain or escalate without executing.

requirements.title: Self-Hosted LLM/VLM Serving and Qualification
requirements.implementation_action: Define exact model/runtime/template/parser/quantization/context stacks, health, versioning, batching, failover, shadow mode, and benchmarks.
requirements.acceptance: Only role- and bucket-qualified versions activate and model changes cannot silently alter accepted decisions.

requirements.title: Unified Runtime Capability Profiles
requirements.implementation_action: Record engine builds, nodes, models, codecs, services, precision, hardware, RAM/VRAM/disk, limits, and measurements.
requirements.acceptance: Routing and scheduling use validated runtime facts and scoped envelopes rather than assumed availability.

requirements.title: Resource-Aware Scheduler and Model Residency
requirements.implementation_action: Control GPU/CPU leases, VRAM admission, concurrency, residency, load/evict, batching, audio isolation, priority, and cancellation.
requirements.acceptance: No pass starts without a certified envelope; starvation, OOM loops, and incompatible co-residency are prevented.
requirements.review_method: media review after degraded or resumed execution

requirements.title: Content-Addressed Artifact and Computation Cache
requirements.implementation_action: Key cache entries by exact package/input/transform/stack/workflow/runtime/configuration hashes and replay policy.
requirements.acceptance: Hits are lineage-safe and stale, revoked, corrupt, stochastic-mismatched, or semantically incompatible artifacts cannot be reused.

requirements.title: Telemetry, Cost, Capacity, and Degraded-Mode Control
requirements.implementation_action: Record latency, utilization, memory, cache, retries, cost, quality, queue depth, service health, and policy-driven degradation.
requirements.acceptance: Degraded routing creates explicit decisions while quality and authority gates remain unchanged.

requirements.title: Modular Multimodal Scorecard
requirements.implementation_action: Define separate identity, morphology, pose, framing, ownership, mask, anatomy, realism, temporal, speech, audio, sync, provenance, and runtime gates.
requirements.acceptance: Metrics have applicability, calibrated thresholds, method, authority, evidence, and severity without one subjective realism scalar.
requirements.review_method: visual and audio metric adjudication sets

requirements.title: Character-to-AV Benchmark Corpus
requirements.implementation_action: Build immutable positive, negative, adversarial, ownership, outage, recovery, cross-engine, specialist, video, audio, and AV fixtures.
requirements.acceptance: The held-out corpus spans solo/multi-character and failure buckets with exact expected outcomes and revisions.
requirements.review_method: independent labeling and full playback review

requirements.title: Calibrated QA Ensemble and Promotion Gate
requirements.implementation_action: Combine deterministic validators, scoped metrics, critics, and review packets without letting weak signals override hard failures.
requirements.acceptance: False accept/reject rates, abstention, disagreement, target/protected/whole-artifact gates, and promotion rules are explicit.

requirements.title: Cross-Wave Regression and Release Readiness Suite
requirements.implementation_action: Verify Rows149-220 preserve Rows001-148 authority, evidence, security, runtime, audio, QA, and completion controls.
requirements.acceptance: Static and selected end-to-end suites pass with residual risk, scope, certificates, blockers, and no false completion recorded.
requirements.review_method: representative image, video, audio, and AV regression review

requirements.title: Operator Information Architecture
requirements.implementation_action: Define Character Library, Scene/Shot, Pose, Passes, Queue, Assets, QA, Blockers, Results, Replay, and Release views.
requirements.acceptance: The UI exposes domain concepts while hiding node IDs, raw paths, credentials, and unsupported internals.

requirements.title: Character, Scene, and Generation Builder UX
requirements.implementation_action: Build package-aware controls for revisions, instances, camera, pose, contact, media, quality, budget, route policy, and constraints.
requirements.acceptance: Requests validate before submission and preview ownership, dependencies, masks, routes, and expected DAG.
requirements.review_method: forms, overlays, timeline, and preview review

requirements.title: Live Run, QA, Blocker, and Repair Console
requirements.implementation_action: Display DAG, routes, attempts, resources, artifacts, metrics, blockers, hypotheses, decisions, revocations, and lineage.
requirements.acceptance: Operators can explain every run/stop/repair without editing a ComfyUI graph and reconnect restores exact state.

requirements.title: App Mode API Binding and UX Qualification
requirements.implementation_action: Bind App Mode to controller APIs, permissions, feature flags, accessibility, performance, reconnect, cancellation, and error states.
requirements.acceptance: The UI cannot bypass schemas/authority and offline, stale, partial, unauthorized, and conflicting states are explicit.
requirements.review_method: complete operator-journey visual and audio review

requirements.title: Phase 0 - Contracts and Authority Freeze
requirements.implementation_action: Implement preservation, governance, IDs, schemas, registries, package fixtures, non-overlap checks, and migration maps before workflow expansion.
requirements.acceptance: Rows149-156 pass static acceptance and downstream work binds canonical versioned contracts.
requirements.review_method: package reference and voice fixture review

requirements.title: Phase 1 - Character-to-Image Vertical Slice
requirements.implementation_action: Implement Character Factory, shot/pose ownership, routing, bridging, specialist passes, Mode A MaskFactory, and one single-character image run.
requirements.acceptance: Rows157-184 pass for one published character, accepted work is reused, and an injected child failure preserves its parent.
requirements.review_method: full character, pose, mask, specialist, and final-image review

requirements.title: Phase 2 - Multi-Character and Multimodal Expansion
requirements.implementation_action: Add two-character ownership/contact, video, integrated audio, AV, durable orchestration, runtime controls, and Mode B only when certified.
requirements.acceptance: Rows161-212 pass required multimodal benchmarks and unqualified Mode B remains draft-only without blocking Mode A paths.
requirements.review_method: mandatory two-character image and complete short AV review

requirements.title: Phase 3 - Autonomous Operator Release Certification
requirements.implementation_action: Qualify self-hosted roles, recovery, resource policy, QA calibration, App Mode, security, release, rollback, and main-task integration.
requirements.acceptance: Rows149-219 are traceable; release/done gates pass; all new files are preserved and adopted by the main task with limitations recorded.
requirements.review_method: final independent image, video, audio, AV, and operator-journey review
